title: Stress Relief (Part 2)

jokes.text: I have now memorized all of your names. Shirty, mole, lazy eye, Mexico, baldy.
jokes.setup: Michael claims to have memorized everyone's names as part of his success recipe
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just using insulting physical descriptors instead of actual names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott moment - his obliviousness to how inappropriate this is while thinking he's being professional. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Hi, Kelly. Screw you. Excuse me. Screw you, too. You forgot her birthday.
jokes.setup: Casual greeting between Kelly and Ryan
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile exchange over forgotten birthday
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing their dysfunctional relationship. Economy is excellent but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Juvie.
jokes.setup: Long explanation about Kelly's time at Berks County Youth Center
jokes.punchline: Ryan's single-word clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that cuts through corporate euphemism. Ryan's matter-of-fact delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: Two. I didn't eat lunch. I didn't eat all my lunch.
jokes.setup: Kevin seemingly counting something
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's tracking ice cream after not finishing lunch, with escalating justification
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin - childlike logic and food obsession. The escalating justification is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: She's hypo-allergenic, she doesn't struggle when you try to dress her, she's a third-generation show cat. Her father was in Meet the Parents.
jokes.setup: Angela announcing a new addition to the Martin family
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's an expensive pedigreed cat with Hollywood connections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela - treating cats like children and obsessing over status. The Meet the Parents reference is absurd but earned.

jokes.text: How much? $7,000. For a cat? I can get you a kid for that.
jokes.setup: Angela reveals the cat cost $7,000
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact offer about human trafficking
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed - delivers something deeply disturbing with complete casualness. Perfect economy and shocking enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: I sold Andy's engagement ring on eBay.
jokes.setup: Question about where Angela got $7,000
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals she sold her ex-fiancé's ring
jokes.explanation: Shows Angela's ruthless practicality and complete lack of sentiment. Delivered with her characteristic matter-of-fact tone.

jokes.text: Seven grand. I've got to see that little bitch.
jokes.setup: Learning about the expensive cat
jokes.punchline: Using 'bitch' as both insult and technically correct term for female cat
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith crudeness with a clever double meaning. The pun works but isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: this company still doesn't recognize cat maternity. I mean, somebody has a kid, oh, sure, take off a year.
jokes.setup: Angela explaining why she can't take time off for her new cat
jokes.punchline: Comparing cat adoption to human maternity leave as if they're equivalent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela absurdity - treating cats exactly like human children. The indignation sells it.

jokes.text: She's right. I had my second kid just for the vacation.
jokes.setup: Angela complaining about maternity leave policies
jokes.punchline: Stanley casually admitting to having a child for selfish reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered Stanley dark humor - completely deadpan confession of terrible parenting motivation.

jokes.text: Because their office is only accessible by cross-country skis.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Nashua doesn't get many visitors
jokes.punchline: Absurd exaggeration about the office's remote location
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor but not particularly character-specific. Could be said by anyone.

jokes.text: My boyfriend dumped me, so I stole his boat. He told me it was his boat. It was actually his father's.
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining her juvenile detention
jokes.punchline: The crime escalates from stealing boyfriend's boat to stealing his father's boat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kelly's impulsiveness and poor judgment. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: I just thought it'd be really romantic, like Thelma & Louise, but with, like, a boat.
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining her motivation for boat theft
jokes.punchline: Comparing grand theft to a romantic movie, missing that Thelma & Louise ends tragically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - romanticizing criminal behavior and missing the dark implications of her reference point.

jokes.text: I thought you said yesterday was your birthday.
jokes.setup: Kelly claiming it's her birthday after emotional confession
jokes.punchline: Jim calling out her inconsistency about when her birthday is
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Kelly's manipulative behavior. Jim's dry delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: I hate it. How do you hate it? It's a cake.
jokes.setup: Jim presents birthday cake to Kelly
jokes.punchline: Kelly immediately rejects it despite Jim's reasonable point
jokes.explanation: Shows Kelly's impossible-to-please nature and Jim's exasperation. Simple but effective character work.

jokes.text: I forgot if there was an 'E' between the 'L' and the 'Y.' I still don't know.
jokes.setup: Kelly asking if they know how to spell her name
jokes.punchline: Jim's honest admission that he's still unsure about spelling 'Kelly'
jokes.explanation: Jim's deadpan honesty about not knowing how to spell a simple name is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: It's birthday. Frosting. Those aren't themes.
jokes.setup: Kelly demanding to know the cake's theme
jokes.punchline: Jim's desperate, nonsensical answers and Kelly's rejection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Jim flails trying to satisfy Kelly's unreasonable demands.

jokes.text: Nice job on the cake, bozo. Okay, you know what? Next time, I'll let you get the cake and I get to scream at the birthday girl.
jokes.setup: Dwight criticizing Jim's cake choice
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic offer to switch roles, revealing the lose-lose situation
jokes.explanation: Shows the impossible situation and Jim's dry frustration with office dynamics.

jokes.text: She's not here? But if you need to get in touch with her immediately, you could talk to A.J. He's a salesman here. A.J. Yeah, he's her boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for Holly at Nashua
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Holly has a boyfriend who works there
jokes.explanation: The cringe comes from audience knowing this will devastate Michael. Well-executed dramatic irony.

jokes.text: She has a boyfriend. I'm so sorry, Michael.
jokes.setup: Learning about Holly's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Michael's visible devastation and Pam's sympathetic reaction
jokes.explanation: More dramatic than comedic, but the awkwardness provides some uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: And then she'll move back to Scranton, and her boyfriend will die.
jokes.setup: Pam encouraging Michael about Holly potentially missing him
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the boyfriend's death as part of the plan
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark humor from usually sweet Pam. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That one ugly cat is humping Princess Lady. No! Awesome. Stop it, Mr. Ash! Bad cat, that is very bad.
jokes.setup: Watching Angela's expensive cat on nanny cam
jokes.punchline: Expensive show cat being mounted by ugly cat while Angela frantically objects
jokes.explanation: Classic visual comedy with Angela's horror contrasted with Kevin's enthusiasm. Physical comedy gold.

jokes.text: Is this how you are with Pam? Because she must want to shoot herself in the face.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for help with party planning
jokes.punchline: Dwight's brutal assessment of Jim's behavior in relationships
jokes.explanation: Dwight's characteristic bluntness applied to Jim's relationship. The imagery is vivid and harsh.

jokes.text: Good morning, Viet-Nashua!
jokes.setup: Michael starting his presentation in Nashua
jokes.punchline: Combining 'Good Morning Vietnam' with Nashua
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that relies on pop culture reference without adding much. Very Michael but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Frankly, my dear, I don't give a damn about paper, and get them to show us the money?
jokes.setup: Michael describing difficult clients
jokes.punchline: Mixing Gone with the Wind and Jerry Maguire quotes about paper sales
jokes.explanation: Michael's typical movie quote mishmash. Feels forced and doesn't land strongly.

jokes.text: A.J. What kind of name is A.J.? What, do you race cars?
jokes.setup: Learning the salesman's name is A.J.
jokes.punchline: Michael's oddly specific assumption about initials meaning race car driver
jokes.explanation: Random but specific Michael logic. The race car assumption is oddly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Does it feel good? Does what feel good? Your life.
jokes.setup: Michael's increasingly personal questions to A.J.
jokes.punchline: Culminating in this existential, hostile question
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cringe - completely inappropriate question delivered with genuine hurt. The pause makes it perfect.

jokes.text: That was weird, huh? It's all part of the presentation.
jokes.setup: Michael's complete breakdown during presentation
jokes.punchline: Pretending his emotional collapse was intentional
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate attempt to save face after completely losing it. Classic character move.

jokes.text: Sales is like a box of chocolates. You never know which vendor you're gonna get.
jokes.setup: Michael attempting to salvage his presentation
jokes.punchline: Mangling the Forrest Gump quote to be about vendors instead of life
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael movie quote butchering. Not particularly clever but in character.

jokes.text: Blazer, freckles, penguin, K.D. Lang, Holly's boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Michael demonstrating mnemonic devices for remembering names
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's categorizing A.J. as 'Holly's boyfriend' rather than learning his name
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Michael's inability to separate personal and professional. The K.D. Lang reference is random perfection.

jokes.text: I have a chainsaw! Cutting down the competition.
jokes.setup: Michael pulling out a chainsaw during sales presentation
jokes.punchline: Explaining it's a metaphor for cutting down competition
jokes.explanation: Michael's over-the-top visual aid. The absurdity works but isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Where is that bad cat? You know who you are. Excuse me, Petals, I'm looking for Mr. Ash. He's a bad cat. Bad, bad cat.
jokes.setup: Angela searching the office for the cat
jokes.punchline: Talking to cats as if they're misbehaving children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela cat obsession. The scolding tone applied to animals is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Is she cleaning the cat with her tongue?
jokes.setup: Watching Angela on the nanny cam
jokes.punchline: Angela has apparently started licking her cat clean
jokes.explanation: Deeply disturbing visual that crosses a line. The horror in Jim's voice sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: The psychological issues that go behind licking a cat are not things I want to go into. Also, I'm pretty sure she coughed up a hairball.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on what he witnessed Angela doing
jokes.punchline: Adding the detail about the hairball
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from already disturbing image. The hairball detail pushes it over the edge.

jokes.text: I went through Holly's things. What? I stole a sleeve of her sweater. I also stole something off of her computer. A document called 'Dear Michael.'
jokes.setup: Michael admitting to going through Holly's belongings
jokes.punchline: Escalating from sweater theft to finding a personal document
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's increasingly inappropriate behavior. The sweater sleeve detail is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: our ideal party consists of beer, fights to the death, cupcakes, blood pudding, blood, touch football, mating, charades and, yes, horse hunting.
jokes.setup: Jim listing Dwight's party suggestions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly violent and bizarre party activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdity filtered through Jim's exasperation. The escalation from cupcakes to mating is brilliant.

jokes.text: Here's one. It was dark, warm, wet. A sudden burst of light, an intense pressure like I'd never felt before. Father dressed in white pulls me forward, mother bites the cord.
jokes.setup: Jim asking Dwight about a birthday he remembers loving
jokes.punchline: Dwight describes his own birth in graphic detail
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight weirdness - describing birth as his favorite birthday. The medical details and 'mother bites the cord' are perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Schrutes don't celebrate birthdays, idiot. It started as a Depression-era practicality and then moved on to an awesome tradition that I look forward to every year.
jokes.setup: Jim asking about Dwight's birthday experiences
jokes.punchline: Dwight saying he looks forward to not celebrating birthdays every year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paradox - looking forward to not celebrating. The Depression-era justification is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Hey, you know what's even cooler than triceratops? Every other dinosaur that ever existed.
jokes.setup: Jim sharing a fond birthday memory about getting a triceratops
jokes.punchline: Dwight's brutal dismissal of triceratops
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight response - completely deflating Jim's nice memory with brutal efficiency. The absoluteness makes it funny.

jokes.text: Why is there a Chiclet on my cake? That's the best part. That represents a pillow. Or a television.
jokes.setup: Kelly noticing strange decoration on cake
jokes.punchline: Jim's explanation that a piece of gum represents either sleeping or TV
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd party planning logic. The Chiclet as dual-purpose symbol is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Because the fun part is you get to decide on an hour of television or an hour of napping. That's our theme.
jokes.setup: Explaining the cake decoration
jokes.punchline: Revealing the theme is choosing between TV and napping
jokes.explanation: Clever solution to impossible party planning situation. The theme is wonderfully lazy and practical.

jokes.text: Can she pick a half hour of each? No. No.
jokes.setup: Phyllis suggesting a compromise on the TV/nap choice
jokes.punchline: Jim and Dwight's unified rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how arbitrary rules become sacred. The unified rejection is funny.

jokes.text: I'm too excited to sleep.
jokes.setup: Kelly choosing nap over TV
jokes.punchline: Immediately saying she's too excited to sleep
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly contradiction. Chooses nap but can't actually nap - very characteristic.

jokes.text: Birthday time is over. Now go make up for all the work you missed when you were taking your nap.
jokes.setup: Hour-long birthday celebration ending
jokes.punchline: Angela's immediate return to harsh taskmaster mode
jokes.explanation: Classic Angela - briefly tolerates fun then immediately returns to strictness. Very in character.

jokes.text: Who have I wronged? Who have I wronged? Oh! Oh! What about that fat guy from Stamford that I insulted? We should find him.
jokes.setup: Michael feeling good about getting closure
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking of someone else he needs to apologize to while still being insulting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - wanting to apologize while still being offensive. Shows he hasn't actually learned anything.

jokes.text: Jabba the Hutt, Pizza the Hut. Fat guys like pizza, pepperoni pizza, Pepperoni Tony.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to remember Tony from Stamford
jokes.punchline: Chain of fat-related associations leading to 'Pepperoni Tony'
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's offensive memory system still in action. The word association is characteristically awful.

jokes.text: You know what? Forget it. I know me. When I saw him I would never be able to apologize to him. Too fat. Big, fat fatty.
jokes.setup: Michael planning to apologize to Tony
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning the plan while continuing to insult Tony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-awareness combined with complete lack of self-control. Shows he knows his flaws but can't overcome them.

tmdb_overview: Stress Relief continues: the office holds a roast of Michael that leaves him devastated.
